description: Languages F-10 (Background Language pathway) capability progression map. Two strands: Communicating and Understanding. Labels are generic and use 'the target language' so the file can be reused across languages.
subject: Languages

_notes.generic_structure: Labels use 'the target language' so the file can be reused across languages. Keep the ACARA codes unchanged unless your renderer explicitly supports code substitution.
_notes.foundation: Foundation has 3 undifferentiated codes before the communicating/understanding sub-strand structure continues across bands.

standards.title: Recognise and communicate meaning
standards.description: with support, recognise and communicate meaning in the target language
standards.capability_goal: Students recognise and communicate meaning in the target language with support.

standards.title: Explore language features
standards.description: explore, with support, language features of the target language noticing similarities and differences between the target language and English
standards.capability_goal: Students explore language features of the target language, noticing similarities and differences with English.

standards.title: Explore language and culture connections
standards.description: explore connections between language and culture
standards.capability_goal: Students explore connections between language and culture.

standards.title: Interact in classroom routines and introductions
standards.description: interact in classroom-related routines and personal introductions
standards.capability_goal: Students interact in classroom-related routines and personal introductions.

standards.title: Collaborate in play-based activities
standards.description: collaborate with others in a range of play-based activities using modelled expressions and visual and spoken cues
standards.capability_goal: Students collaborate with others in play-based activities using modelled expressions and cues.

standards.title: Locate key information in texts
standards.description: locate, with support, key information in spoken and written texts, and respond using gestures, images, words and modelled sentences
standards.capability_goal: Students locate key information in spoken and written texts and respond using gestures, images, words and modelled sentences.

standards.title: Recognise cultural meaning in interaction
standards.description: recognise that language carries cultural meaning in everyday social interactions
standards.capability_goal: Students recognise that language carries cultural meaning in everyday social interactions.

standards.title: Create texts with familiar language
standards.description: with support, create spoken, written and multimodal texts, using familiar words, modelled phrases and sentences, copying known characters and Pinyin
standards.capability_goal: Students create spoken, written and multimodal texts using familiar words, modelled phrases and sentences, copying known characters and Pinyin.

standards.title: Recognise sound patterns and tones
standards.description: recognise and use sound patterns and tones of the target language
standards.capability_goal: Students recognise and use sound patterns and tones of the target language.

standards.title: Recognise meaning-making features
standards.description: recognise that target language components and/or characters and features of language are used to construct meaning
standards.capability_goal: Students recognise that components, characters and features of language are used to construct meaning.

standards.title: Recognise similarities and differences with English
standards.description: recognise that the target language has features that may be similar to or different from English
standards.capability_goal: Students recognise that the target language has features that may be similar to or different from English.

standards.title: Notice language reflects cultural practices
standards.description: notice that people use language in ways that reflect cultural practices
standards.capability_goal: Students notice that people use language in ways that reflect cultural practices.

standards.title: Initiate and respond in familiar contexts
standards.description: initiate exchanges and respond to questions about home and classroom environments, using familiar expressions
standards.capability_goal: Students initiate exchanges and respond to questions about home and classroom environments using familiar expressions.

standards.title: Plan and transact with others
standards.description: participate in activities that involve planning and transacting with others, using a range of phrases, structures and modelled sentences in familiar contexts
standards.capability_goal: Students participate in planning and transacting with others using a range of phrases, structures and modelled sentences.

standards.title: Locate, organise and respond to information
standards.description: locate, organise and respond to key information related to familiar content in spoken, written and multimodal texts
standards.capability_goal: Students locate, organise and respond to key information in spoken, written and multimodal texts.

standards.title: Interpret language conveying cultural meaning
standards.description: develop strategies to interpret language that conveys cultural meaning
standards.capability_goal: Students develop strategies to interpret language that conveys cultural meaning.

standards.title: Create spoken, written and multimodal texts
standards.description: create and present informative and imaginative spoken, written and multimodal texts, using a range of sentence structures and modelled textual conventions, characters appropriate to context, and/or Pinyin
standards.capability_goal: Students create and present informative and imaginative spoken, written and multimodal texts using sentence structures, conventions, characters and/or Pinyin appropriate to context.

standards.title: Recognise and use tonal features
standards.description: recognise and use the tonal features of the target language in different contexts and understand how syllables are represented
standards.capability_goal: Students recognise and use tonal features in different contexts and understand how syllables are represented.

standards.title: Recognise writing system and syntax features
standards.description: recognise and use features of the target language writing system, familiar components and/or characters, a range of sentence structures and basic syntax, in familiar texts and contexts
standards.capability_goal: Students recognise and use features of the writing system, familiar components or characters, sentence structures and syntax in familiar texts and contexts.

standards.title: Recognise and compare spoken and written features
standards.description: recognise and compare features of familiar spoken and written text in the target language and English
standards.capability_goal: Students recognise and compare features of familiar spoken and written text in the target language and English.

standards.title: Identify language and cultural connections
standards.description: identify connections between target language and cultural practices
standards.capability_goal: Students identify connections between target language and cultural practices.

standards.title: Initiate and sustain exchanges
standards.description: initiate and sustain exchanges related to students’ experiences and perspectives
standards.capability_goal: Students initiate and sustain exchanges related to their experiences and perspectives.

standards.title: Plan and negotiate with others
standards.description: participate in activities that involve planning and negotiating with others, using modelled and idiomatic language, to agree, suggest and resolve
standards.capability_goal: Students participate in planning and negotiating with others using modelled and idiomatic language to agree, suggest and resolve.

standards.title: Locate and process information and ideas
standards.description: locate and process information and ideas in a range of spoken, written and multimodal texts, and respond in different ways to suit purpose and audience
standards.capability_goal: Students locate and process information and ideas in a range of texts and respond in different ways to suit purpose and audience.

standards.title: Interpret and translate in cultural contexts
standards.description: apply strategies to interpret and translate language in non-verbal, spoken and written cultural contexts
standards.capability_goal: Students apply strategies to interpret and translate language in non-verbal, spoken and written cultural contexts.

standards.title: Create texts for purpose and audience
standards.description: create and present informative and imaginative spoken, written and multimodal texts, appropriate to purpose and audience, using a range of sentence structures to sequence information and ideas, and textual conventions
standards.capability_goal: Students create and present informative and imaginative texts appropriate to purpose and audience using sentence structures and textual conventions.

standards.title: Use tone, intonation, stress and phrasing
standards.description: use tone-syllables, intonation, stress and phrasing to express feelings and opinions
standards.capability_goal: Students use tone, intonation, stress and phrasing to express feelings and opinions.

standards.title: Use writing system and syntax features
standards.description: identify and use components and/or characters, sentence structures, syntax and writing system features to compose and respond to familiar and some unfamiliar texts and contexts
standards.capability_goal: Students identify and use components or characters, sentence structures, syntax and writing system features to compose and respond to texts.

standards.title: Compare structures using metalanguage
standards.description: compare some target language structures and features with those of English, using some familiar metalanguage
standards.capability_goal: Students compare some target language structures and features with English using familiar metalanguage.

standards.title: Recognise language reflects culture and identity
standards.description: recognise that language reflects cultural practices, values and identity, and that this impacts on non-verbal and spoken communication
standards.capability_goal: Students recognise that language reflects cultural practices, values and identity, and that this impacts communication.

standards.title: Initiate and sustain exchanges in varied contexts
standards.description: initiate and sustain exchanges in familiar and unfamiliar contexts related to students’ experiences, feelings and views, adjusting their language in response to others
standards.capability_goal: Students initiate and sustain exchanges in familiar and unfamiliar contexts, adjusting language in response to others.

standards.title: Collaborate through transacting and negotiating
standards.description: collaborate in activities that involve the language of transacting, negotiating and justifying to plan events
standards.capability_goal: Students collaborate in activities involving transacting, negotiating and justifying to plan events.

standards.title: Interpret and analyse ideas in texts
standards.description: interpret and analyse information, ideas and opinions in a range of spoken, written and multimodal texts, and respond appropriately to cultural context, purpose and audience
standards.capability_goal: Students interpret and analyse information, ideas and opinions in texts and respond appropriately to cultural context, purpose and audience.

standards.title: Interpret and translate across contexts
standards.description: interpret and translate non-verbal, spoken and written language to convey meaning in a range of familiar and unfamiliar cultural contexts
standards.capability_goal: Students interpret and translate non-verbal, spoken and written language to convey meaning in familiar and unfamiliar cultural contexts.

standards.title: Create texts for context, purpose and audience
standards.description: create and present spoken, written and multimodal informative and imaginative texts, manipulating language to suit context, purpose and audience
standards.capability_goal: Students create and present spoken, written and multimodal informative and imaginative texts, manipulating language to suit context, purpose and audience.

standards.title: Use tonal and spoken features for fluency
standards.description: use tone-syllables and apply intonation, stress and phrasing to develop fluency, and respond to and create texts in a range of contexts
standards.capability_goal: Students use tonal and spoken features to develop fluency and respond to and create texts in a range of contexts.

standards.title: Apply writing system and syntax knowledge
standards.description: apply knowledge of character form and function, sentence structures, syntax, and writing system features to infer meaning and to compose a range of texts
standards.capability_goal: Students apply knowledge of character form and function, sentence structures, syntax and writing system features to infer meaning and compose texts.

standards.title: Reflect on similarities and differences
standards.description: reflect on and explain similarities and differences between the target language and English language structures and features, using metalanguage
standards.capability_goal: Students reflect on and explain similarities and differences between the target language and English language structures and features using metalanguage.

standards.title: Explain identity shaped by language and culture
standards.description: reflect on and explain how their own and others’ identity is shaped by language(s), culture(s), attitudes, beliefs and values
standards.capability_goal: Students reflect on and explain how identity is shaped by language, culture, attitudes, beliefs and values.

standards.title: Initiate, sustain and extend exchanges
standards.description: initiate, sustain and extend exchanges in a range of contexts, responding to ideas, opinions and perspectives
standards.capability_goal: Students initiate, sustain and extend exchanges in a range of contexts, responding to ideas, opinions and perspectives.

standards.title: Contribute to discussions with diverse views
standards.description: contribute to discussions that involve diverse views to negotiate outcomes, address issues and compare cultural experiences
standards.capability_goal: Students contribute to discussions involving diverse views to negotiate outcomes, address issues and compare cultural experiences.

standards.title: Analyse and evaluate ideas across texts
standards.description: analyse and evaluate information, ideas and perspectives in a broad range of spoken, written and multimodal texts and respond appropriately to cultural context, purpose and audience
standards.capability_goal: Students analyse and evaluate information, ideas and perspectives in a broad range of texts and respond appropriately to cultural context, purpose and audience.

standards.title: Interpret and translate to explain meaning
standards.description: interpret and translate a range of non-verbal, spoken and written texts, and explain how meaning is conveyed to reflect cultural context, purpose and audience
standards.capability_goal: Students interpret and translate a range of texts and explain how meaning is conveyed to reflect cultural context, purpose and audience.

standards.title: Create texts for diverse contexts and audiences
standards.description: create and present informative and imaginative texts for diverse contexts and purposes, selecting vocabulary, expressions, sentence structures and textual features and conventions to engage different audiences
standards.capability_goal: Students create and present informative and imaginative texts for diverse contexts and purposes, selecting vocabulary, expressions, sentence structures and conventions to engage different audiences.

standards.title: Apply spoken features and conventions for fluency
standards.description: apply features and conventions of spoken the target language to extend fluency in responding to and creating texts appropriate to contexts, purposes and audiences
standards.capability_goal: Students apply features and conventions of spoken language to extend fluency in responding to and creating texts appropriate to contexts, purposes and audiences.

standards.title: Apply writing system and grammatical knowledge
standards.description: apply knowledge of character form and function, and complex grammatical structures and features, to predict meaning and to compose a wide range of texts
standards.capability_goal: Students apply knowledge of character form and function and complex grammatical structures to predict meaning and compose a wide range of texts.

standards.title: Analyse texts using metalanguage
standards.description: analyse a range of target language texts, using metalanguage to explain linguistic, textual and cultural elements
standards.capability_goal: Students analyse a range of target language texts using metalanguage to explain linguistic, textual and cultural elements.

standards.title: Evaluate identity and communication
standards.description: reflect on and evaluate how their own and others’ identity is shaped by language(s), culture(s), attitudes, beliefs and values, and how these affect ways of communicating
standards.capability_goal: Students reflect on and evaluate how identity is shaped by language, culture, attitudes, beliefs and values, and how these affect ways of communicating.

capability_threads.description: Languages F-10 Background Language capability sequences.

communicating.label: Communicating meaning in the target language

mediating.label: Mediating meaning in and between languages

creating.label: Creating text in the target language

understanding_culture.label: Understanding the interrelationship of language and culture
